# Howl — LLM context file

Howl is a Deno-native full-stack framework (`@hushkey/howl` on JSR) built on top of Fresh 2.x.
It adds a typed API layer, native cookie/header APIs on ctx, React ecosystem compatibility,
built-in middlewares, and a production build pipeline on top of Fresh's routing/SSR/islands.

Root: `packages/` (single JSR package, multiple sub-entries).

---

## Package exports

| Import path | Source | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `@hushkey/howl` | `core/mod.ts` | Runtime — App, Context, routing, islands, SSR |
| `@hushkey/howl/dev` | `dev/mod.ts` | Build pipeline — HowlBuilder, esbuild, HMR |
| `@hushkey/howl/plugins` | `plugins/mod.ts` | Tailwind v4, CSS Modules |
| `@hushkey/howl/api` | `api/mod.ts` | defineApi, defineConfig, caching, OpenAPI |
| `@hushkey/howl/api/errors` | `api/errors.ts` | API error types |
| `@hushkey/howl/api/cache` | `api/cache/mod.ts` | memoryCache, redisCache, tryCache |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware` | `core/middlewares/mod.ts` | Built-in middlewares — coalesceRequests, compression, cors, csrf, csp, staticFiles, trailingSlashes |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/coalesce` | `core/middlewares/coalesce.ts` | Request coalescing (thundering-herd protection) |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/compression` | `core/middlewares/compression.ts` | Gzip/deflate compression |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/cors` | `core/middlewares/cors.ts` | CORS middleware |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/csrf` | `core/middlewares/csrf.ts` | CSRF middleware |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/csp` | `core/middlewares/csp.ts` | Content-Security-Policy middleware |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/trailing-slashes` | `core/middlewares/trailing_slashes.ts` | Trailing slash middleware |
| `@hushkey/howl/middleware/static-files` | `core/middlewares/static_files.ts` | Static file serving |
| `@hushkey/howl/runtime` | `core/runtime/shared.ts` | Client-safe runtime (Partial, IS_BROWSER, asset, Head) — must point to `shared.ts`, not `client/mod.ts` (latter calls `document.addEventListener` at module level) |

---

## Project file conventions

| Item | Location | Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| App root | `client/pages/_app.tsx` | Root HTML shell — receives `{ Component }` |
| Layout | `client/pages/_layout.tsx` | Shared UI layout — receives `{ Component }` |
| Pages | `client/pages/**/*.tsx` | Receives `Context<State>` directly as param |
| Islands | `client/islands/**/*.island.tsx` | Hydrated on client; `export const howl = { ssr: false }` skips SSR |
| Endpoint contracts | `server/apis/**/*.api.ts` | Type-safe endpoints — auto-crawled |
| Middleware | `server/middleware/**/*.middleware.ts` | Runs before routes |
| Static files | `static/**/*` | Served as-is |
| Config | `howl.config.ts` | State interface, roles, defineApi factory |
| Dev entrypoint | `dev.ts` | HowlBuilder setup |
| Server entrypoint | `server/main.ts` | App + middleware setup |
| Build output | `dist/` | Production build |

---

## App setup

```typescript
// howl.config.ts
import { defineConfig, memoryCache, redisCache, tryCache } from "@hushkey/howl/api";

export interface State {
  userContext?: { user?: { id: string; roles: Role[] } };
}
export const roles = ["user", "admin"] as const;
export type Role = typeof roles[number];

export const { defineApi, config: apiConfig } = defineConfig<State, Role>({
  roles,
  cache: tryCache(memoryCache({ maxSize: 1000 }), redisCache(redis)),
  checkPermissionStrategy: (ctx, allowedRoles) => {
    const user = ctx.state.userContext?.user;
    if (!user) return ctx.json({ message: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
    if (!allowedRoles.some((r) => user.roles.includes(r))) {
      return ctx.json({ message: "Forbidden" }, { status: 403 });
    }
    // return nothing = allow
  },
});
```

```typescript
// server/main.ts
import { Howl, staticFiles } from "@hushkey/howl";
import { coalesceRequests, compression } from "@hushkey/howl/middleware";
import { apiConfig } from "../howl.config.ts";
import { middleware } from "./middleware/_index.middleware.ts";

export const app = new Howl<State>({ logger: true });

app.use(coalesceRequests()); // thundering-herd protection — must be first
app.use(compression());
app.use(staticFiles());
app.configure(middleware);
app.fsApiRoutes(apiConfig); // crawls server/apis/, registers all .api.ts
app.fsClientRoutes();       // crawls client/pages/, mounts all routes

export default { app };
```

```typescript
// dev.ts
import { HowlBuilder } from "@hushkey/howl/dev";
import { tailwindPlugin } from "@hushkey/howl/plugins";
import { app } from "./server/main.ts";

const builder = new HowlBuilder<State>(app, {
  root: import.meta.dirname ?? "",
  importApp: () => app,
  outDir: "dist",
  serverEntry: "./server/main.ts",
  clientEntry: "./client/pages/_app.ts",
});

tailwindPlugin(builder.getBuilder("default")!);

if (Deno.args.includes("build")) {
  await builder.build();
} else {
  await builder.listen();
}
```

---

## Context API

`Context<State>` is created fresh per request — fully volatile, never shared across requests.

```typescript
// Properties
ctx.req        // Request
ctx.url        // URL
ctx.params     // Record<string, string> — path params
ctx.state      // State — shared mutable object, reset per request
ctx.route      // string | null — matched pattern
ctx.error      // unknown | null
ctx.isPartial  // boolean — Fresh partial request
ctx.info       // Deno.ServeHandlerInfo

// Response helpers (all merge ctx.headers automatically)
ctx.json(data, init?)
ctx.text(content, init?)
ctx.html(content, init?)
ctx.render(<JSX />, init?, layoutConfig?)
ctx.redirect(path, status?)
ctx.stream(iterable, init?)

// Query params
ctx.query()        // Record<string, string> — all params
ctx.query("key")   // string | undefined — single param

// Cookies
ctx.cookies.get("name")
ctx.cookies.set("name", value, { httpOnly: true, sameSite: "Strict" })
ctx.cookies.delete("name")

// Response headers (auto-merged into every response including renders)
ctx.headers.set("X-Request-Id", crypto.randomUUID())

// Middleware chain
ctx.next()
```

---

## Pages

```typescript
// client/pages/index.tsx
// Pages receive Context<State> directly as param (not a props object)
export default function Index(ctx: Context<State>) {
  return <h1>Hello {ctx.state.userContext?.user?.id}</h1>;
}

// With handler returning data
export const handler = (ctx: Context<State>) => page({ user: "leo" });

export default function Index({ data }: PageProps<{ user: string }, State>) {
  return <h1>Hello {data.user}</h1>;
}
```

---

## Islands

```typescript
// client/islands/counter.island.tsx
import { useState } from "preact/hooks";

export const howl = { ssr: false }; // optional — skip SSR

export default function Counter() {
  const [n, setN] = useState(0);
  return <button onClick={() => setN(n + 1)}>{n}</button>;
}
```

React libs (sonner, react-query, etc.) work transparently via preact/compat alias — no config needed.

---

## Endpoint contracts

Each `.api.ts` file is a self-contained typed endpoint. Drop the file, it's live — no wiring.

```typescript
// server/apis/public/ping.api.ts
import { defineApi } from "../../../howl.config.ts";
import { z } from "zod";

export default defineApi({
  name: "Ping",
  directory: "public",       // OpenAPI tag; auto-path: /api/public/ping
  method: "GET",
  roles: [],                 // [] = public; ["admin"] = role-gated
  caching: { ttl: 5 },       // seconds
  query: z.object({
    page: z.string().optional(),
    limit: z.string(),
  }),
  responses: {
    200: z.object({ ok: z.boolean(), message: z.string() }),
  },
  handler: (ctx) => {
    const { limit } = ctx.query(); // typed
    return { statusCode: 200, ok: true, message: `pong — limit ${limit}` };
  },
});
```

```typescript
// With request body
export default defineApi({
  name: "Sign In",
  directory: "authentication",
  method: "POST",
  roles: [],
  requestBody: z.object({ email: z.string().email(), password: z.string().min(8) }),
  responses: {
    200: z.object({ data: z.object({ token: z.string() }) }),
    401: z.object({ message: z.string() }),
  },
  handler: async (ctx) => {
    const { email, password } = ctx.req.body; // fully typed
    return { statusCode: 200, data: { token: "jwt..." } };
  },
});
```

**Auto-path:** `directory + name` → `/api/{directory}/{kebab-case-name}`
**Explicit path:** `path: "/api/v1/public/ping"` or `path: ["/a", "/b"]` overrides auto-path.

**Handler return shape:** `{ statusCode, ...fields }` → response body: `{ ok: true, data: { ...fields } }`
Raw `Response` return is also supported for full control.

---

## File-system routing

The filesystem is the authoritative source for all API paths. Explicit `path` in the definition overrides; `directory + name` auto-generation is bypassed. `[param]` brackets become `:param`.

Within each folder the crawler sorts entries before loading: static files (no brackets) first, `[param]` files last, directories after files. This guarantees static routes are always registered before dynamic ones. The router also independently checks the static map before iterating dynamic patterns, so literal paths can never be shadowed by a param segment.

```
server/apis/
├── public/ping.api.ts                        → /api/public/ping
├── admin/
│   └── [table]/
│       ├── index.api.ts                      → /api/admin/:table
│       ├── [id].api.ts                       → /api/admin/:table/:id
│       └── restore.api.ts                    → /api/admin/:table/restore
└── authentication/
    └── oauth/
        ├── callback.api.ts                   → /api/authentication/oauth/callback  (static — registered first)
        └── [provider].api.ts                 → /api/authentication/oauth/:provider (dynamic — registered after)
```

```typescript
// server/apis/admin/[table]/[id].api.ts
export default defineApi({
  name: "Get Row",
  directory: "admin",
  method: "GET",
  roles: ["admin"],
  params: z.object({ table: z.string(), id: z.string() }), // required for validation + OpenAPI
  responses: { 200: z.object({ row: z.any() }) },
  handler: (ctx) => {
    const { table, id } = ctx.params; // typed
    return { statusCode: 200, row: null };
  },
});
```

---

## OpenAPI

Auto-generated from all registered `.api.ts` files. Expose wherever you want:

```typescript
import { getApiSpecs } from "@hushkey/howl/api";

app.get("/api/docs", (ctx) => ctx.json(getApiSpecs()));
// or gated:
app.get("/api/docs", requireRole("admin"), (ctx) => ctx.json(getApiSpecs()));
```

Returns `null` before routes are registered, `OpenAPIV3_1.Document` after.

---

## Built-in middleware

```typescript
import {
  coalesceRequests,
  compression,
  cors,
  csp,
  csrf,
  staticFiles,
  trailingSlashes,
} from "@hushkey/howl/middleware";

app.use(coalesceRequests());                              // thundering-herd protection — must be first
app.use(compression());                                   // gzip/deflate for text, JSON, JS, SVG
app.use(staticFiles());
app.use(cors({ origin: "https://myapp.com", credentials: true }));
app.use(csrf());                                          // blocks cross-origin state-mutating requests
app.use(csp({ reportOnly: false, reportTo: "/api/csp" })); // sensible defaults + overrides
app.use(trailingSlashes("never"));                        // "always" | "never"
```

`coalesceRequests()` deduplicates concurrent GET requests to the same URL. Only applies to requests without a `Cookie` or `Authorization` header — authenticated requests always run their own handler.

---

## Caching

```typescript
import { memoryCache, redisCache, tryCache } from "@hushkey/howl/api/cache";

memoryCache({ maxSize: 1000 })          // in-process LRU, zero deps
redisCache(redisClient)                 // any ioredis-compatible client (auto error listener — reconnect events don't crash)
tryCache(primary, fallback)             // primary first, fallback on miss/error
tryCache(primary, fallback, { timeoutMs: 150 }) // fallback if primary slow
```

Cache key: `{method}:{url}:{userId}` — per-user automatically when authenticated. Implement `CacheAdapter` to plug in any other backend.

---

## Error handling

```typescript
import { HttpError } from "@hushkey/howl";

throw new HttpError(404, "Not found");
throw new HttpError(401);
throw new HttpError(403, "Forbidden");
```

Auto-caught and returned as JSON with the correct HTTP status.

---

## Time constants

```typescript
import { SECOND, MINUTE, HOUR, DAY, WEEK } from "@hushkey/howl";
// Values in milliseconds
```

---

## Howl class

```typescript
const app = new Howl<State>({
  logger: true,   // timestamps + PID on all console output
  debug: true,    // enables console.debug
});

// Middleware
app.use(...middlewares);

// Manual routes
app.get("/path", handler);
app.post("/path", middleware, handler);
app.put("/path", handler);
app.patch("/path", handler);
app.delete("/path", handler);

// FS route discovery
app.fsClientRoutes();        // crawls client/pages/
app.fsApiRoutes(apiConfig);  // crawls server/apis/

// Grouped middleware
app.configure(middlewareFn);
```

---

## HowlBuilder

```typescript
const builder = new HowlBuilder(app, {
  root?: string,               // project root (default: Deno.cwd())
  outDir?: string,             // output dir (default: "_howl")
  serverEntry?: string,        // relative path to server main.ts
  importApp?: () => app | { app },
  alias?: Record<string, string>,
  plugins?: EsbuildPlugin[],
});

await builder.listen(options?);  // dev server with HMR
await builder.build();           // production build

builder.getBuilder("default");   // access inner Builder for plugin setup
builder.getApis();               // all discovered AnyApiDefinition[]
builder.registerIsland(specifier);
```

---

## Stack

- Deno 2.x
- Fresh 2.x (vendored + extended)
- Preact 10.x + preact/compat (React transparency)
- Zod 4.x for all validation
- esbuild 0.25.x for bundling
- Tailwind CSS v4 via PostCSS
- OpenTelemetry API for tracing
